mechanism_narrative: Persistently low effective federal funds rates compress the equity risk premium by reducing the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, leading to positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative on average during months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, or the rank correlation between FEDFUNDS levels and returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points signals tightening labor markets and accelerating wage income, which drives consumption and produces positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the mean return in triggered windows is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicates broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate revenues more than either variable alone, generating positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132989 and RSAFS at 408039 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: A bivariate model with the interaction term explains less than 1% incremental R-squared versus additive main effects over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal weakening supply-side momentum and impending negative earnings revisions, leading to negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes in late-cycle regimes produce positive average 20-day returns, or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in consumer prices exceeding 0.4% raises expectations of near-term monetary tightening and erodes real corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: Months with triggered inflation spikes show positive average 5-day returns, or the rank IC between inflation surprises and 5-day returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2% annualized signals durable aggregate demand expansion that translates into sustained earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: High-growth quarters fail to deliver positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the average forward return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in housing starts exceeding 2% reflects strengthening construction activity and positive multiplier effects across the economy, generating positive S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases month-over-month by more than 2%
failure_condition: Strong housing-start months do not exhibit positive average 5-day returns, or the rank IC between HOUST changes and 5-day returns is negative and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly increases in industrial production signal a sustained manufacturing upswing that boosts corporate operating leverage and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the mean return in triggered windows is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).
